name: Valley National Bank
description_short: Regional bank established in 1927 offering checking, savings, lending, and investment products across 200+ branches in six states with over $63 billion in assets.
description_long: Valley National Bank has operated as a trusted financial institution since 1927, establishing itself as a leading commercial and retail bank in the United States. The company has grown to manage over $63 billion in assets and maintains more than 200 consumer and commercial banking branches across six states, employing over 3,800 people. The bank serves both personal and commercial customers with a focus on relationship-based banking and community presence. Valley offers a comprehensive suite of banking products for personal customers, including multiple checking and credit card options, savings accounts, money market accounts, and various CD terms with promotional rates (currently advertising 4.00% APY on 9-month CDs). For lending needs, the bank provides auto loans, mortgages with low down payment options, home equity loans and lines of credit, and refinancing services. Additional offerings include retirement accounts (IRAs), complimentary online and mobile banking platforms, and personalized service through expert relationship managers at physical branch locations. The bank differentiates itself through its established 97-year history, extensive branch network for in-person support, FDIC insurance protection up to $250,000 per account category, and recent industry recognition. In 2025, Valley received recognition from Newsweek as one of America's Best Regional Banks, was named Best Bank in New Jersey by GOBankingRates, and won an ADP Culture at Work award for workplace innovation. The company emphasizes straightforward products and personalized expert support rather than specialty or fintech-focused services. Valley is a solid choice for consumers seeking traditional banking with strong local presence and established credibility, though the website provides limited detail on specific APRs for checking/savings products, fee structures, or minimum balance requirements. As a regional bank with primarily brick-and-mortar operations, it may have less competitive digital capabilities compared to newer fintech banks, and its service area is limited to six states rather than nationwide availability.
services: Checking accounts with multiple options for different needs and life stages
Savings accounts and money market accounts with flexible terms
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with fixed rates and various maturity options
Credit cards with cashback, rewards, or low introductory APR options
Auto loans for vehicle purchases and lease buyouts
Mortgage products including low down payment options
Home equity loans and lines of credit
Mobile banking and online banking platform
Bill pay and money management tools
In-person banking at 200+ branch locations with expert relationship managers
pros: 200+ physical branches across six states for in-person banking and relationship management
FDIC insurance coverage up to $250,000 per depositor for each account category
Over $63 billion in total assets providing financial stability and resources
Established since 1927 with 97 years of banking history and customer trust
2025 recognition as one of America's Best Regional Banks by Newsweek and as Best Bank in New Jersey
Mobile app and online banking platform for account management from anywhere
Current CD promotion at 4.00% APY with flexible term options and $500 minimum
Wide range of lending products including auto loans, mortgages, and home equity solutions
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to only six states, not nationwide coverage
Website does not clearly display APRs, interest rates, or fee schedules for checking/savings accounts
Minimum account opening requirements and balance thresholds not specified on main website
Regional bank with primarily branch-based model may have less advanced digital capabilities than fintech competitors
No mention of specialized products like investment advisory services or wealth management specifics on consumer pages
best_for: Consumers in Valley's six-state footprint seeking traditional banking with physical branch access
Customers who value established institutions with 97-year track records and local community presence
People looking for straightforward checking, savings, and lending products with personal relationship manager support
Savers interested in CDs and money market accounts with FDIC protection and fixed-rate options
diagnosis: Valley National Bank is best for traditional consumers in its six-state service area who value established institutions, in-person branch support, and straightforward banking products backed by FDIC insurance and $63+ billion in assets. The main caveat is that specific product rates, fees, and minimum balance requirements are not detailed on the public website, requiring direct contact with branches or account comparison research before opening accounts.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Regional bank established in 1927 with 200+ branches offering checking, savings, lending, and investments.
